## Origins

Lord [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) of [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End) was one of the leaders of the [rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion) that overthrew King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen), the Mad King, ending the Targaryen Dynasty.(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_fall_of_the_dragons:_robert.E2.80.99s_rebellion.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0) Following the defeat of the Targaryen forces and Aerys's death in [283 AC](wiki:283_AC), Robert was crowned and reigned as king over all the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragotappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) Securing his power, Robert wed [Cersei](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) of [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) in [284 AC](wiki:284_AC) following the advice of his foster-father and [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_glorious_reign.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-5) Within a few years the royal succession was secured by three heirs: [Joffrey](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon) (b. [286 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest)), [Myrcella](wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon) (b. [290 AC](wiki:290_AC)), and [Tommen](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) (b. [291 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest)).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Cal_6-0) However, whilst Robert and the realm believed Cersei's three children to have been fathered by Robert, they had actually been fathered by [Ser](wiki:Knight) [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), Queen Cersei's twin brother, with whom she had been having an incestuous relationship for years.(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

However, King Robert's brother, [Stannis](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), eventually became suspicious of the legitimacy of Robert’s three children. He shared his suspicions with Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), still serving as the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King). Together, they investigated the case and concluded that the children were not Robert's, but the product of incest between Queen Cersei and her twin brother Jaime. Before they are able to address the issue to the King, however, Lord Arryn unexpectedly dies. While King Robert traveled [North](wiki:North) to appoint a new Hand of the King, Stannis, suspecting that Arryn had been murdered by the Lannisters because of their discovery regarding Cersei’s children, fled to [his own stronghold](wiki:Dragonstone) on the island of [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone_(island)).

Thus, Robert remained oblivious to these suspicions concerning his children, and traveled with a large part of the royal court to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) to appoint his old friend, Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), as Arryn's successor as Hand of the King.(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) Lord Eddard had originally considered to turn down the offer, but changed his mind upon the arrival of a secret message from [Lysa Arryn](wiki:Lysa_Arryn), Jon Arryn's widow and the sister to Lord Eddard's wife, [Catelyn](wiki:Catelyn_Stark). Lysa claimed that the Lannisters had murdered her husband, and causing Eddard to feel forced to accept Robert's appointment, as to be able to investigate Arryn's death and the involvement of [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) Upon Robert’s suggestion, Eddard also accepts to a betrothal between his daughter [Sansa](wiki:Sansa_Stark) and Prince [Joffrey Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-1)(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1)

While the royal court is at Winterfell, Lord Eddard's younger son [Bran](wiki:Bran_Stark) accidentally witnesses a romantic moment between Queen Cersei and Ser Jaime, and is thrown from a tower window to silence him.(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Bran's condition is severe, and he lies comatose for several weeks.(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) After the royal court (including Bran's father, Lord Eddard, and his sisters [Sansa](wiki:Sansa_Stark) and [Arya](wiki:Arya_Stark)) have left Winterfell for [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), an assassin attempts to murder Bran in his bed. He is halted by Bran's mother, [Catelyn](wiki:Catelyn_Tully), injuring her in the process, before being killed by Bran's direwolf pet, [Summer](wiki:Summer).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) To inform her husband of the attempt on Bran's life, Catelyn travels south to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), where her childhood friend [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) claims that the assassin's weapon used belonged to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), the younger brother of Queen Cersei.(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot14.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-2)(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

While Lord Eddard begins investigating Arryn's death, Catelyn crosses paths with Tyrion Lannister at the [crossroads inn](wiki:Crossroads_inn). Deciding to arrest him, Catelyn charges him with the attempt on Bran's life, which leads to the start of conflicts in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)

Meanwhile, in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), Eddard’s investigation into Arryn's death has eventually independently led him to discover the truth about Queen Cersei's children. He confronts her about the matter, offering her the option to flee, which she refuses.(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1) Eddard intends to share his discoveries with the king when Robert returns from his [boar](wiki:Boar) hunt. However, Robert returns having been fatally wounded by the boar, in a hunting incident which Cersei had orchestrated).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0) Robert's younger brother and [master of laws](wiki:Master_of_laws), [Renly](wiki:Renly_Baratheon), unaware of the illegitimacy of Cersei's children, proposes to Eddard that they take Cersei's children hostage, as to prevent the Lannisters from seizing power after Robert's death. Eddard refuses him, however, and Renly departs the city for [Highgarden](wiki:Highgarden), the seat of [House Tyrell](wiki:House_Tyrell).(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0)

After Robert's death, Eddard attempts a coup, sharing with the small council that Robert's brother [Stannis](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), and not [Joffrey](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon), is Robert's true heir. He is betrayed by [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) and the [City Watch](wiki:City_Watch_of_King's_Landing), however, and arrested and imprisoned by the Lannisters.(wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings.cite_ref-Ragot49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-1)

## Origins

[House Tyrell](wiki:House_Tyrell) aligns itself with King [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) at the start of the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings), in [299 AC](wiki:299_AC), with Renly marrying [Margaery Tyrell](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Ragot69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0) Following [Renly's death](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(299)), the [small council](wiki:Small_council) sends Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) to negotiate an alliance between Houses Tyrell and [Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister), supporting the claim of King [Joffrey Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Racok36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) After the [Battle of the Blackwater](wiki:Battle_of_the_Blackwater), Joffrey discards [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) and is betrothed to Margaery.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Racok65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) set the first day of the new year, [300 AC](wiki:300_AC), as the date for the great wedding, so that the ceremony herald the dawn of a new era.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-6)

Ser [Dontos Hollard](wiki:Dontos_Hollard) tells Sansa she will be able to escape captivity during the upcoming wedding, and he gives her a silver hair net decorated with black [amethysts](wiki:Amethyst) from [Asshai](wiki:Asshai).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Racok65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) Dontos insists that Sansa wear the hair net during the wedding.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

After arriving in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), Margaery's grandmother, [Olenna Tyrell](wiki:Olenna_Tyrell), questions Sansa about Joffrey to see if the stories of the boy king's cruelty are true.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Instead of Joffrey, Sansa is forced by Tywin Lannister to marry [Tyrion](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), Joffrey's uncle and Tywin's son.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

[Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) overhears the [ghost of High Heart](wiki:Ghost_of_High_Heart) telling the [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners) that "she dreamt of a maid at a feast with purple serpents in her hair, venom dripping from their fangs".(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

## Aftermath

A drunken Ser [Dontos Hollard](wiki:Dontos_Hollard) helps [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) escape the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep), and [Oswell Kettleblack](wiki:Oswell_Kettleblack) rows them into [Blackwater Bay](wiki:Blackwater_Bay). When they reach a waiting ship, however, Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish) has Dontos killed by Ser [Lothor Brune](wiki:Lothor_Brune)'s men.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos61.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) Petyr and Sansa sail on *[Merling King](wiki:Merling_King)* to the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers). Once at [Petyr's tower](wiki:Drearfort), Sansa learns that her silver hair net secretly contained poison within its amethysts. [Olenna Tyrell](wiki:Olenna_Tyrell) took the poison from an unsuspecting Sansa when she straightened the girl's hair during the celebration. Sansa determines that the poison was then put into Joffrey's wine, and Petyr explains he had plotted Joffrey's death so that [Margaery Tyrell](wiki:Margaery_Tyrell) could marry Joffrey's younger brother, [Tommen Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) According to [George R. R. Martin](wiki:George_R._R._Martin), the conspirators wanted Joffrey to appear to die accidentally by choking, rather than a public assassination like the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-EW_1-1)

[Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), Sansa's husband, is also falsely implicated. While in custody, he believes that Sansa had poisoned Joffrey with other conspirators.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) At the start of his trial, Tyrion states that Joffrey had accidentally choked to death on the pie.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-1) Maesters [Ballabar](wiki:Ballabar) and [Frenken](wiki:Frenken) find no food in Joffrey's throat and concur that he was poisoned, however, and Grand Maester [Pycelle](wiki:Pycelle) testifies that Tyrion killed the king with the [strangler](wiki:Strangler).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-2) Lady [Taena Merryweather](wiki:Taena_Merryweather) swears she saw Tyrion drop something into Joffrey's [wedding chalice](wiki:Wedding_chalice), and [Lord Estermont](wiki:Lord_Estermont), [Josmyn Peckledon](wiki:Josmyn_Peckledon), [Galyeon of Cuy](wiki:Galyeon_of_Cuy), and [Morros](wiki:Morros_Slynt) and [Jothos Slynt](wiki:Jothos_Slynt) all recall having seen Tyrion empty the chalice of wine while the king died.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-3)

Tyrion eventually insists upon [trial by combat](wiki:Trial_by_combat), but he is found guilty after Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) kills [Oberyn Martell](wiki:Oberyn_Martell).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos70.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) has Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys) help him free Tyrion from the [black cells](wiki:Black_cells) the night before his execution, however. After Jaime tells his brother about [Tysha](wiki:Tysha), the angry Tyrion falsely claims responsibility for Joffrey's death. Before escaping the castle, Tyrion kills his father, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos77.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)

[Smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) tell various stories about Joffrey's death, including that Tyrion slit the boy's throat with a dagger and drank his blood from a chalice,(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) or that Sansa killed Joffrey with a [spell](wiki:Magic) and then escaped the Red Keep by turning into a [wolf](wiki:Wolf) with [bat](wiki:Bat) wings.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos74.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)

With the exception of his mother, [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister), Joffrey is not particularly mourned by anyone; even his biological father, Jaime, feels that Joffrey deserved his fate.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos72.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0) Tyrion states that Joffrey would have become a worse king than the Mad King, [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos77.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-1) Ser [Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) thinks that the only good thing that could be said of Joffrey was that he was tall and strong for his age.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0) With poison in mind, Jaime, the [Lord Commander of the Kingsguard](wiki:Lord_Commander_of_the_Kingsguard), orders Ser [Boros Blount](wiki:Boros_Blount) to taste all food for King Tommen I.(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Rasos67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0) The twice-widowed Margaery eventually [marries Tommen](wiki:Wedding_of_Tommen_I_Baratheon_and_Margaery_Tyrell).(wiki:Purple_Wedding.cite_ref-Raffc12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0)

## Origins

War breaks out after the death of King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) and the arrest of Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark) in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0)(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0) [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) invades the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands), scattering the riverlords and placing [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun) under siege. Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) occupies the [crossroads](wiki:Crossroads) near the [ruby ford](wiki:Ruby_ford) and waits to see how Houses [Arryn](wiki:House_Arryn) and [Stark](wiki:House_Stark) will respond. Tywin expects [House Frey](wiki:House_Frey), who control the crossing over the [Green Fork](wiki:Green_Fork), to remain neutral.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-0)

[Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) calls the [northern](wiki:North) banners and marches south from [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) to rescue his father.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-0) When Ser [Addam Marbrand](wiki:Addam_Marbrand)'s outriders report that Robb is on the [causeway](wiki:Causeway), Tywin orders for Addam to draw the northmen further south while Tywin leads his own host north on the [kingsroad](wiki:Kingsroad) east of the Green Fork to meet them.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-1)

Once south of the [Neck](wiki:Neck), Robb's mother, [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark), persuades Lord [Walder Frey](wiki:Walder_Frey) to ally with Robb, opening the way over the Green Fork at the [Twins](wiki:Twins). Robb, who does not wish to stake everything on one battle with Tywin, splits his host in two. The foot and one-tenth of the horse under Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton) continue marching south on the kingsroad to engage Tywin, while Robb himself commands the rest of the cavalry and crosses west of the river, in the hope of lifting the siege of Riverrun by taking Tywin's son, Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), unawares.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-1)(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot59.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-1)

Addam's outriders inform Tywin that the Freys have joined the Starks and their combined host is marching south on the kingsroad.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-19) Ser [Brynden Tully](wiki:Brynden_Tully)'s men kill some of Addam's scouts, however, preventing the Lannisters from knowing that Robb's host split at the Twins.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot59.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-2)

## Narrative Arc

When the [westermen](wiki:Westerlands) think the [northmen](wiki:North) are still a day's march away, the [Stark](wiki:House_Stark) army, commanded by Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton), marches throughout the night in an effort to take the [Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) army by surprise, arriving before dawn.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-20)

However, the Lannister army arrays itself in time to meet the northmen. The westermen's order of battle includes:

- Ser [Kevan Lannister](wiki:Kevan_Lannister) commands ten thousand men in the center, with Lords [Lefford](wiki:Leo_Lefford), [Lydden](wiki:Lewys_Lydden) and [Serrett](wiki:House_Serrett) at his side with three hundred heavy horse. His foot archers are arranged in three long lines east and west of the [kingsroad](wiki:Kingsroad). Between the archers are squares of pikemen, and behind them are ranks of [men-at-arms](wiki:Men-at-arms) wielding axes, swords, and spears.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-21)
- On the right, Ser [Addam Marbrand](wiki:Addam_Marbrand) commands four thousand knights and other heavy lancers, among them Ser [Flement Brax](wiki:Flement_Brax) and members of Houses [Crakehall](wiki:House_Crakehall), [Swyft](wiki:House_Swyft), and other families.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-22)
- Ser [Gregor Clegane](wiki:Gregor_Clegane) commands the vanguard on the left. At one thousand men, the van is entirely mounted, but consists of freeriders, [sellswords](wiki:Sellswords), and raw fieldhands and smallfolk from [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport). The van also includes [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), his sellsword [Bronn](wiki:Bronn), and his three hundred [Vale mountain clansmen](wiki:Vale_mountain_clans),(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-23) who are considered savages by the nobles.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-2)
- From the high ground of a hilltop, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) commands the reserve of five thousand men, half mounted, half afoot.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-24)

Unbeknownst to Tyrion, Tywin has placed his undisciplined men on the left to tempt the Stark commander, whom Tywin believes is the untested [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark), to overcommit there. Tywin hopes the Lannister van will rout and the Starks will plunge into the gap, eager to flank him. Tywin then intends for Kevan and the center to wheel to the left and take the northerners in the flank, driving them into the Green Fork.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-25) Before the fighting begins, Gregor orders Tyrion to hold the van's left, along the riverfront.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-26)

Kevan's archers fire volleys of arrows from the kingsroad as the northmen march forward; Tyrion observes the banners of Houses [Cerwyn](wiki:House_Cerwyn), [Frey](wiki:House_Frey), [Glover](wiki:House_Glover), [Hornwood](wiki:House_Hornwood), [Karstark](wiki:House_Karstark), and Stark. The armored veterans with Gregor break a shield wall of Karstark spearmen, and Tyrion's clansmen follow through the breach. Tyrion notices a volley of arrows land amongst the northmen and Lannister van, although he does not know from where they were fired. Tyrion kills a northern swordsman who attacks him, and then forces a northern knight to yield after the man becomes trapped beneath his horse.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-27)

Meanwhile, Kevan sends in his pikemen to push the Stark lines against the hills behind them, and Kevan's archers fire against the northmen behind shield walls. Tywin leads his reserve behind the van, along the river, to break the remnants of the Stark line.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-28) Tywin feels the northmen were led cautiously during the battle, which he did not expect from young Robb.(wiki:Battle_on_the_Green_Fork.cite_ref-Ragot62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-29)

## Origins

### Tourney at Harrenhal

In [281 AC](wiki:281_AC), a grand [tourney](wiki:Tourney_at_Harrenhal) was held by Lord [Walter Whent](wiki:Walter_Whent) at [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal).(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot58.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Many nobles from all over the Seven Kingdoms came to the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) to take part in the festivities. Amongst them were the heir to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell), [Brandon Stark](wiki:Brandon_Stark), and his younger siblings, [Eddard](wiki:Eddard_Stark), [Lyanna](wiki:Lyanna_Stark) and [Benjen](wiki:Benjen_Stark).(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rasos24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

The most prominent visitor of the tourney was King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen), however. Aerys had not left the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) for many years, ever since his half-year capture during the [Defiance of Duskendale](wiki:Defiance_of_Duskendale) in [277 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest),(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_targaryen_kings:_aerys_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) but he was inspired to come to the tourney by words from his [master of whisperers](wiki:Master_of_whisperers), the eunuch [Varys](wiki:Varys).(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Radwd67.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

The tourney was cause for great celebration, as spring had come to Westeros, or so men thought.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-12) In addition, all of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) had gathered to welcome their newest member, Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister). However, the tourney's ending was less joyful. After winning the final tilt, Prince [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen) was to crown the new [queen of love and beauty](wiki:Queen_of_love_and_beauty). Instead of crowning his wife, the Dornish princess [Elia Martell](wiki:Elia_Martell), Rhaegar rode past her and crowned Lyanna Stark of Winterfell instead. Eddard Stark would later recall this moment as "the moment when all smiles died".(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot58.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)

### Abduction of Lyanna Stark

At the coming of the new year, [Lyanna Stark](wiki:Lyanna_Stark) was abducted by Prince [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen) near [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal).(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_fall_of_the_dragons:_the_year_of_the_false_spring.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) Her brother, [Brandon Stark](wiki:Brandon_Stark), was on his way to [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun) to wed [Catelyn Tully](wiki:Catelyn_Stark), when the news reached him. Brandon rode at once to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) with his companions: [Ethan Glover](wiki:Ethan_Glover), Brandon's squire, [Elbert Arryn](wiki:Elbert_Arryn), nephew to Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn) and the heir of the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie), [Kyle Royce](wiki:Kyle_Royce), and [Jeffory Mallister](wiki:Jeffory_Mallister).(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) Brandon rode for the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep), and outside its gates, unaware that Rhaegar was not present, shouted for him to come out and die. King [Aerys II](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen) had them all arrested for plotting the murder of the crown prince, and summoned the fathers of all those who stood accused to court.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1) Lord [Rickard Stark](wiki:Rickard_Stark), who had been on his way south with the wedding party for Brandon's wedding,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rawoiaf_catelyn_tully.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) went to King's Landing with two hundred men.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) None of them returned north. In King's Landing, all of those who stood accused were executed,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-1) with the sole exception of Ethan Glover.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0)

King Aerys did allow for trials, of a sort. Lord Rickard Stark demanded a [trial by battle](wiki:Trial_by_battle), and the king granted his request. Rickard dressed for battle, but Aerys declared fire to be the champion of [House Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen). He had Rickard suspended from the rafters in the throne room, where his [pyromancers](wiki:Pyromancers), [Rossart](wiki:Rossart) amongst them, kindled a blaze beneath the Lord of Winterfell. Brandon Stark was brought in as well, and placed in a [Tyroshi](wiki:Tyrosh) strangling device, while a longsword was placed just outside his reach. Brandon was allowed to try and free his father, but the more he struggled to reach the sword, the tighter the cord around his neck became. Brandon strangled himself in an attempt to reach the sword and save his father, while Lord Rickard cooked in his armor.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-2)

Next, Aerys demanded that Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn) send him the heads of Lord [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_Baratheon) of [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End), Lyanna's betrothed,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) and [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), now the Lord of Winterfell after the deaths of his father and older brother.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_fall_of_the_dragons:_robert.27s_rebellion.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0) Robert and Eddard were Jon's wards at the Eyrie, however, and Jon instead raised his banners in revolt.(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-1) This act is seen as the start of the war,(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_the_fall_of_the_dragons:_robert.27s_rebellion.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-1) which would last "close to a year".(wiki:Robert's_Rebellion.cite_ref-Ragot12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-3)

## Origins

After the unexpected death of the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) journeys north to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) with the royal family and half his court, to ask his boyhood friend, Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), to be his new Hand.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Eddard agrees and journeys south to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot12.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

Once in the capital, Lord Eddard investigates the death of his predecessor.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) He finds out that the king's three children, Prince [Joffrey](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon), Princess [Myrcella](wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon), and Prince [Tommen Baratheon](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon), are in fact [bastards](wiki:Bastard) from Queen [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister)'s affair with her twin brother, Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard).(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

Lord Stark decides to tell the truth to his friend, but he is too late as Robert is dying due to an hunting accident in the [kingswood](wiki:Kingswood) with a [boar](wiki:Boar).(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) On his deathbed, Robert names Eddard [Protector of the Realm](wiki:Protector_of_the_Realm) and [Lord Regent](wiki:Regent). Eddard alters Robert's will, changing Joffrey's name for "the heir".(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)

Eddard intends for his daughters, [Sansa](wiki:Sansa_Stark) and [Arya](wiki:Arya_Stark), to escape King's Landing on *[Wind Witch](wiki:Wind_Witch)*, and he instructs [Tomard](wiki:Tomard) with personally delivering a letter to [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), [Lord of Dragonstone](wiki:Lord_of_Dragonstone), during the ship's voyage to the [north](wiki:North).(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2) Sansa, who is betrothed to Prince Joffrey, informs Cersei that she is going to be sent away.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0)

While Robert lies dying, Lord [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) approaches Eddard and suggests they seize control of the royal children before Queen Cersei can strike.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-3) Eddard refuses the offer, however, and instead meets with Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish), [master of coin](wiki:Master_of_coin). Littlefinger agrees to bribe the [City Watch of King's Landing](wiki:City_Watch_of_King's_Landing) so that [Janos Slynt](wiki:Janos_Slynt) and his gold cloaks will support Eddard against Cersei.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-4)

## Aftermath

Lord Stark is imprisoned in the [black cells](wiki:Black_cells) of the Red Keep.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot58.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) Sansa is brought before Cersei in the small council chambers on the third day. The queen informs Sansa that her father was a traitor who tried to steal Joffrey's throne, showing her Eddard's letter to Stannis. The council has Sansa, who still desires to wed Joffrey, write letters urging her family to remain peaceful. Lord Baelish takes custody of Jeyne.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-2)

Eddard's eldest son and heir, [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark), calls the [banners](wiki:Bannerman) of the [north](wiki:North) to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell),(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0) and he marches south to rescue his father.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) Joffrey's grandfather, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), invades the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) from the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands).(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)

In Joffrey's first court session, Tywin is appointed Hand of the King.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0) Janos is named [Lord of Harrenhal](wiki:Lord_of_Harrenhal), creating [House Slynt](wiki:House_Slynt), and he is given an advisory seat on the small council.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-1) Barristan is dismissed from the Kingsguard, with Cersei's brother Jaime promoted to Lord Commander in his place. Though not a [knight](wiki:Knight), Sandor Clegane is sworn in as member of Joffrey's Kingsguard, taking Barristan's slot.(wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark.cite_ref-Ragot57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-2)

## Origins

[Viserys Targaryen](wiki:Viserys_Targaryen) and his sister [Daenerys](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) have lived in exile in the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities) in the aftermath of [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion). While the exiles are staying with [Illyrio Mopatis](wiki:Illyrio_Mopatis) in [Pentos](wiki:Pentos), the [magister](wiki:Magister) negotiates a [betrothal](wiki:Betrothal) between Daenerys and [Drogo](wiki:Drogo), the greatest [khal](wiki:Khal) of the [Dothraki sea](wiki:Dothraki_sea). In return, Drogo will help Viserys recover the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) from the [Usurper](wiki:Usurper), King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon).(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)
Daenerys is to meet Drogo for the first time at a feast held at [his manse](wiki:Drogo's_manse). Illyrio has the girl wear a [silk](wiki:Silk) gown of deep plum, gilded sandals, a tiara, [golden](wiki:Gold) bracelets crusted with [amethysts](wiki:Amethyst), and a heavy golden torc emblazoned with [Valyrian](wiki:Valyria) [glyphs](wiki:High_Valyrian), to emphasize the fact that she is a princess.(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) Other notables in attendance for the meeting are Khal [Moro](wiki:Moro) and his son [Rhogoro](wiki:Rhogoro), the brother of the [Archon of Tyrosh](wiki:Archon_of_Tyrosh), and the exiled [knight](wiki:Knight) Ser [Jorah Mormont](wiki:Jorah_Mormont).(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot3.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) Viserys accepts Jorah's offer of [service](wiki:Sworn_sword) that night.(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)
After Drogo agrees to marry Daenerys, he allows the siblings to live in his manse while he joins his [khalasar](wiki:Khalasar) outside of Pentos.(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3) The city's magisters double the [city guard](wiki:City_watch_(Pentos)) while the [Dothraki](wiki:Dothraki) are nearby.(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)
Viserys, while willing to wed Daenerys to Drogo to achieve his goals, is resentful of having to give her up, and the night before the wedding tries to enter her room and claim her [maidenhead](wiki:Maidenhead). Illyrio, however, has the foresight to post guards on her room and ensure the impetuous Viserys does not undo "[years of planning](wiki:Illyrio's_plans)".(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Radwd5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

## Aftermath

After the wedding, Drogo's khalasar departs Pentos for [Vaes Dothrak](wiki:Vaes_Dothrak) so that Daenerys can be presented to the [dosh khaleen](wiki:Dosh_khaleen).(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-10)(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) Illyrio invites Viserys to remain with him in Pentos, but Viserys insists upon accompanying Drogo to ensure the khal honors their alliance.(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2) Drogo initially ignores Daenerys during the day, but every night, contrary to his behavior at the wedding's consummation, he couples with her roughly, uncaring of her bruises and sores from riding and ignoring her cries of pain, until Daenerys considers suicide. However, Daenerys has a [dragon dream](wiki:Dragon_dream) that gives her new confidence and strength. Eventually, Daenerys initiates sex with Drogo, marking the beginning of a loving relationship between them. On the far end of the Dothraki sea, on her fourteenth nameday, Daenerys reveals that she is pregnant.(wiki:Wedding_of_Drogo_and_Daenerys_Targaryen.cite_ref-Ragot23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-3)

## Aftermath

Now in control of six of the Seven Kingdoms and known as the Conqueror, Aegon I Targaryen announced he would rule not from [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) or [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown), but from the [Aegonfort](wiki:Aegonfort) and the new city growing around it, [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing). Aegon had melted the swords of those who had opposed him into the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne), a reminder of their submission to the Targaryens.(wiki:Aegon's_Conquest.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_reign_of_the_dragons:_the_conquest.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-27) Queen Visenya Targaryen led the suppression of the [Sistermen's Rebellion](wiki:Sistermen's_Rebellion).(wiki:Aegon's_Conquest.cite_ref-Rfabreign_of_the_dragon_-_the_wars_of_king_aegon_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0)

The [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands) fell into chaos after the death of [Harren the Black](wiki:Harren_Hoare). In [2 AC](wiki:2_AC), Aegon led the [invasion of the Iron Islands](wiki:Invasion_of_the_Iron_Islands) and forced the surviving lords of the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) to submit. Aegon allowed them to choose Lord [Vickon Greyjoy](wiki:Vickon_Greyjoy) as the new [Lord of the Iron Islands](wiki:Lord_of_the_Iron_Islands).(wiki:Aegon's_Conquest.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_greyjoys_of_pyke.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0) Dorne remained unconquered, however. Aegon continued the [Wars of Conquest](wiki:Wars_of_Conquest) in [4 AC](wiki:4_AC), beginning the bloody and unsuccessful [First Dornish War](wiki:First_Dornish_War).(wiki:Aegon's_Conquest.cite_ref-Rtwoiafdorne:_dorne_against_the_dragons.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)

## Origins

The rival claims of Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen) and King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen) led to [civil war](wiki:Dance_of_the_Dragons) in the [Seven Kingdoms](wiki:Seven_Kingdoms). Rhaenyra's [blacks](wiki:Blacks) had more [dragons](wiki:Dragon) than Aegon's [greens](wiki:Greens), but most of their strength was focused around [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) instead of mainland [Westeros](wiki:Westeros). With [House Tully](wiki:House_Tully) effectively neutral because of disagreement between Lord [Grover Tully](wiki:Grover_Tully) and his grandson, Ser [Elmo](wiki:Elmo_Tully), Rhaenyra's husband, Prince [Daemon Targaryen](wiki:Daemon_Targaryen), suggested [Harrenhal](wiki:Harrenhal) as a rallying point for potential supporters in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). [Larys Strong](wiki:Larys_Strong), Lord of Harrenhal, was a green supporter of Aegon, however.(wiki:Assault_on_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_blacks_and_the_greens.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)

While [Jacaerys](wiki:Jacaerys_Velaryon) and [Lucerys Velaryon](wiki:Lucerys_Velaryon) left Dragonstone to seek allies and [Corlys Velaryon](wiki:Corlys_Velaryon) closed [Blackwater Bay](wiki:Blackwater_Bay), Rhaenyra allowed her husband to command the assault on Harrenhal.(wiki:Assault_on_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_blacks_and_the_greens.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)

## Aftermath

The loss of Harrenhal shocked Aegon II and encouraged the blacks, who soon after won at the [Burning Mill](wiki:Battle_of_the_Burning_Mill) and [Stone Hedge](wiki:Taking_of_Stone_Hedge).(wiki:Assault_on_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_the_red_dragon_and_the_gold.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:Assault_on_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) [House Blackwood](wiki:House_Blackwood) and other river lords gathered at Harrenhal, which was where Daemon approved of [Blood and Cheese](wiki:Blood_and_Cheese) to avenge the death of Rhaenyra's son, Lucerys.

When Daemon learned that Ser [Criston Cole](wiki:Criston_Cole) and Prince [Aemond Targaryen](wiki:Aemond_Targaryen) had left [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to retake Harrenhal, the blacks abandoned the castle and the rivermen marched to meet the [westermen](wiki:Westermen) of Lord [Jason Lannister](wiki:Jason_Lannister) at the [Red Fork](wiki:Battle_at_the_Red_Fork). While Criston and Aemond retook Harrenhal without bloodshed, their absence contributed to the [fall of King's Landing](wiki:Fall_of_King's_Landing) to Daemon and Rhaenyra. Aemond ordered the executions of [Simon Strong](wiki:Simon_Strong) and his kin, although he spared [Alys Rivers](wiki:Alys_Rivers).(wiki:Assault_on_Harrenhal.cite_ref-Rfabthe_dying_of_the_dragons_-_rhaenyra_triumphant.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

## Origins

Although the uprising began during the reign of King [Aenys I Targaryen](wiki:Aenys_I_Targaryen), the first seeds were already planted during the reign of his father, King [Aegon I Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_I_Targaryen). Before the birth of Aenys's children, his younger half-brother [Maegor](wiki:Maegor_I_Targaryen) was regarded as his heir. However, Aenys was wed in [22 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), and his first child, Princess [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)), was born the next year. Her birth sparked the discussion of the line of succession, as it was unclear whether Maegor remained second in the line of succession or whether he fell behind the newborn Rhaena. In [24 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest), shortly after Maegor turned twelve, his mother Queen [Visenya Targaryen](wiki:Visenya_Targaryen) proposed a betrothal between Rhaena and Maegor to settle the issue of the royal succession. However, both Prince Aenys and [his wife](wiki:Alyssa_Velaryon) and the [High Septon](wiki:High_Septon_(Aegon_I)) protested.(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aenys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) The High Septon suggested his own niece, Lady [Ceryse Hightower](wiki:Ceryse_Hightower), as a bride for the young prince instead. Maegor and Ceryse were subsequently wed in [25 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest) at the [Starry Sept](wiki:Starry_Sept) in [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown), in a ceremony officiated by the High Septon himself.(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_maegor_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

After fourteen years of childless marriage, Maegor suddenly announced in [39 AC](wiki:39_AC) that Ceryse was barren and that he had taken another wife, Lady [Alys Harroway](wiki:Alys_Harroway), the daughter of the new [Lord of Harrenhal](wiki:Lord_of_Harrenhal), [Lucas Harroway](wiki:Lucas_Harroway). Unable to convince the [septon](wiki:Septon) at [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) to perform the wedding, Maegor had taken Alys to wife in a Valyrian wedding ceremony led by the Dowager Queen, Visenya.

Maegor's second marriage angered many. Ceryse's father, Lord [Martyn Hightower](wiki:Martyn_Hightower), protested the marriage to King Aenys and demanded that Maegor set Alys aside. The High Septon denounced the marriage as sin and fornication and called Alys "this whore of Harroway." Many of the pious lords in the realm similarly condemned the marriage, and openly began to call Alys "Maegor's Whore". Maegor remained defiant, however, citing that his father had taken two wives as well, claiming that the strictures of the Faith did not rule [the blood of the dragon](wiki:House_Targaryen).(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabthe_sons_of_the_dragon.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1)

Maegor quarreled bitterly with his brother over the marriage, as the king had neither known of the marriage prior to the ceremony, nor given his leave for it. Eventually, Aenys made Maegor choose between setting Alys aside or going into exile for five years. Maegor chose the latter, and left for [Pentos](wiki:Pentos) in [40 AC](wiki:Years_after_Aegon's_Conquest).(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aenys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) The High Septon was not yet satisfied, however, so Aenys appointed Septon [Murmison](wiki:Murmison) as his new [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), but even he could not heal the rift between the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) and the Faith.(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aenys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2)

The situation worsened in [41 AC](wiki:41_AC), when Aenys wed his daughter [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Aenys_I)) and son [Aegon](wiki:Aegon_Targaryen_(son_of_Aenys_I)) to one another. The High Septon sent Aenys a denunciation, addressing him as "King Abomination". Even the pious lords and [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) who had loved Aenys turned against the king.(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aenys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-3)

## Aftermath

Prince Jaehaerys ascended the throne in [48 AC](wiki:48_AC) following Maegor's death. He was crowned by the High Septon, and he declared an amnesty for the surviving Faith Militant. While he wished to disband the holy orders, Jaehaerys appointed [Joffrey Doggett](wiki:Joffrey_Doggett) to his [kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) where he served faithfully for many years. Septon [Moon](wiki:Moon) and his less reputable rabble of followers marched on [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown) to try to have him formally declared High Septon, but he received no support from major lords. Moon was assassinated in the night and his followers quickly dispersed (many suspect Moon's death was ordered by the Hightowers but accounts differ).

The first dozen years of young Jaehaerys's reign would be spent trying to reunite a realm strained by his uncle's tyranny, though thankfully Jaehaerys proved to be an adept diplomat. Moreover the royal treasury was seriously depleted, so Jaehaerys had to take out massive loans from the [Iron Bank of Braavos](wiki:Iron_Bank_of_Braavos) and then spend years adjusting the tax code to try to pay off the interest without inflaming the lords of the realm to rebellion again.

About a decade into his reign, he became acquainted with [Septon](wiki:Septon) [Barth](wiki:Barth), who tended to the books and records in the Red Keep's library. Jaehaerys soon named him [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), a position he kept for forty years.(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_jaehaerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1)(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rasos54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) Together with Barth, Jaehaerys formally re-conciliated the Iron Throne with the Faith. The Poor Fellows and Warrior's Sons, much reduced in number, were still present and restless, eager to restore their orders. Jaehaerys sent Septon Barth to Oldtown to speak with the High Septon. It was agreed that the Iron Throne would always protect and defend the Faith, if the last few Stars and Swords would put down their weapons, and if the Faith agreed to accept justice from the Iron Throne instead of the Faith.(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_jaehaerys_i.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2) The Faith accepted Targaryen marriage practices in the [Doctrine of Exceptionalism](wiki:Doctrine_of_Exceptionalism).(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rfaba_time_of_testing_-_the_realm_remade.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)

Two years after Jaehaerys ascended the throne, members of the Faith Militant and Maegor's loyalists who had been exiled to the [Wall](wiki:Wall) made common cause in a [rebellion against the leadership](wiki:Night's_Watch_rebellion_of_50_AC) of the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch) to try to establish their own lordships, requiring the intervention of Lord [Walton Stark](wiki:Walton_Stark) to crush the traitors.(wiki:Faith_Militant_uprising.cite_ref-Rfabjaehaerys_and_alysanne_-_their_triumphs_and_tragedies.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0)

## Origins

Having learned that the bulk of the Watch's fighting force has been wiped out in the [fight at the Fist](wiki:Fight_at_the_Fist) by the [Others](wiki:Others), [Mance Rayder](wiki:Mance_Rayder) plans to lead his main host through the gate in the Wall by Castle Black. In order to do so, he first plans to weaken the strength of the brothers of the Night's Watch who still remain at the castle.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos73.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) He sends out several smaller raiding parties, which prompts [Lord Steward](wiki:Lord_Steward) [Bowen Marsh](wiki:Bowen_Marsh) to march, taking most of the fighting men still remaining at Castle Black with him, leaving only the old, the green, and the sickly behind to defend Castle Black. Bowen leaves the infirm Ser [Wynton Stout](wiki:Wynton_Stout) as castellan, although the blacksmith [Donal Noye](wiki:Donal_Noye) takes *de facto* leadership due to Stout's senility.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

Next, Mance sends [Styr](wiki:Styr), the Magnar of [Thenn](wiki:Thenn_(region)), and [Jarl](wiki:Jarl) as joint commanders with a force of a hundred [Thenns](wiki:Thenn_(people)) and twenty of Jarl's raiders to scale the Wall and take the weakly-defended Castle Black unaware from the south.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2) Among Jarl's raiders are the [spearwife](wiki:Spearwife) [Ygritte](wiki:Ygritte), [Errok](wiki:Errok), [Grigg the Goat](wiki:Grigg_the_Goat), [Quort](wiki:Quort), [Bodger](wiki:Bodger), [Hempen Dan](wiki:Hempen_Dan), [Del](wiki:Del), [Henk the Helm](wiki:Henk_the_Helm), [Toefinger](wiki:Toefinger), and [Big Boil](wiki:Big_Boil),(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) as well as [Jon Snow](wiki:Jon_Snow), pretending to have abandoned the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch).(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2) They climb the Wall near [Greyguard](wiki:Greyguard),(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) at a spot where the Wall rises eight hundred feet above the forest floor. Despite the Wall there being about a hundred feet higher than usual, a third of the Wall's total height is earth and stone, making it easier to climb. Under the command of Jarl, Errok, and Grigg three groups of four raiders climb the Wall using spiked boots, hammers, axes, and ropes. After six hours of climbing, Jarl's group touches upon a patch of bad ice at a height of six hundred feet and falls to their deaths.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3) The other eight climbers reach the top of the Wall as the sun starts to set. They hoist up ladders attached to the ropes by the raiders who had remained behind on the ground, allowing the other hundred and eight warriors to climb to the top more easily. Two more Thenns fall to their deaths during the climb. Near midnight the others reach the top of the Wall.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4)

Styr leads his warriors off the Wall at [Greyguard](wiki:Greyguard), an abandoned castle of the Night's Watch where the presence of huge stone steps improve the descent. From there, the group goes deeply into the [Gift](wiki:Gift), passing a few inhabited villages and scattered roundtowers on the way.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1) At [Queenscrown](wiki:Queenscrown), Jon Snow escapes the raiding party and travels back to [Castle Black](wiki:Castle_Black) by the way of [Mole's Town](wiki:Mole's_Town) in time to warn the remaining brothers of the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch) of the on-coming attack from the south.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2)(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2) Three-quarters of the residents of Mole's Town flee to the castle, all of them helping prepare for the assault in some way. Donal Noye and Jon organize the defense around archers in the towers and a line of fighting men on the massive wooden staircase that zigzags up the south face of the Wall. As suggested by Maester [Aemon](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I)), "scarecrow sentinels", dummy soldiers dressed in the clothes of a brother of the Night's Watch, are posted at roofs and windows to inflate their numbers.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0) Further, Aemon sent letters to each of the four kings in the realm, aswell as [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown) and the [Citadel](wiki:Citadel), and half a hundred lords, including many northern lords.(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-Rasos55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)(wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black.cite_ref-9)

## Origins

[Aemon](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I)), the maester at [Castle Black](wiki:Castle_Black), sends [ravens](wiki:Raven) seeking aid for the [Night's Watch](wiki:Night's_Watch) after the disastrous [fight at the Fist](wiki:Fight_at_the_Fist), and Lord [Davos Seaworth](wiki:Davos_Seaworth) informs King [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) at [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone).(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Radwd54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0)

Having learned that the bulk of the Watch's fighting force has been wiped out at the Fist by [Others](wiki:Others) and their [wights](wiki:Wights), [Mance Rayder](wiki:Mance_Rayder) plans to lead his main host of [free folk](wiki:Free_folk) through the gate in the [Wall](wiki:Wall) at Castle Black. He lures the main strength still remaining at the castle away by having smaller raiding parties show up at multiple places at the Wall, leaving Castle Black to be weakly defended.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0) Next, Mance sends [Styr](wiki:Styr), the Magnar of [Thenn](wiki:Thenn_(valley)), and [Jarl](wiki:Jarl) as joint commanders with a force of a hundred [Thenns](wiki:Thenns) and twenty of Jarl's raiders to scale the Wall and take Castle Black unaware from the south.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0) Jarl dies during the ascent,(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0) however, and the survivors descend using the abandoned castle of [Greyguard](wiki:Greyguard).(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3) They travel through the [Gift](wiki:Gift) to Castle Black.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0) While at [Queenscrown](wiki:Queenscrown), [Jon Snow](wiki:Jon_Snow), a black brother who had pretended to have deserted the Night's Watch, escapes the raiding party.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-1) He travels back to Castle Black by the way of [Mole's Town](wiki:Mole's_Town) in time to warn the remaining brothers of the Watch of the on-coming attack from the south.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-1)

[Donal Noye](wiki:Donal_Noye) and [Jon Snow](wiki:Jon_Snow) repel the [attack on Castle Black](wiki:Attack_on_Castle_Black) led by [Styr](wiki:Styr).(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-0) Since the Thenns failed to capture the castle and open the gate for the [King-Beyond-the-Wall](wiki:King-Beyond-the-Wall)'s main host, Mance is forced to attack the Wall in an attempt to breach the gate.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)

Meanwhile, the fleet of [Salladhor Saan](wiki:Salladhor_Saan) transports Stannis's army north to [Eastwatch-by-the-Sea](wiki:Eastwatch-by-the-Sea).(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-0) [Cotter Pyke](wiki:Cotter_Pyke) leads Stannis's knights west on [ranger](wiki:Ranger)'s roads close to the Wall.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos75.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-5)

## Narrative Arc

### Battle for the gate

Mance has his host encamped half a mile north from the Wall.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) The host is first noticed in the black of the night, when the sentries of the Night's Watch at Castle Black blow their horns two times to alert the brothers of the presence of the [free folk](wiki:Free_folk). Per the command of [Donal Noye](wiki:Donal_Noye), barrels of burning pitch are thrown down the Wall by trebuchet in order to get a view of the enemy. Next, the brothers set ablaze a dozen jars of lamp oil, which they shove over the edge one by one, followed by a barrel of pitch.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)

Donal takes two archers and two spearmen with him down the Wall to hold the gate from within the tunnel, leaving Jon Snow in command of the forces above. From atop the Wall, the brothers blindly loose arrows and throw rocks with small catapults. After several hours, one of the trebuchets breaks down, while the free folk down below learn to avoid the places the rocks of the other trebuchet land. They fight blindly all throughout the night.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)

When the sun has come up, the free folk bring in a ram. The wildling line is centered by a hundred or more [mammoths](wiki:Mammoth) with [giants](wiki:Giants) on their backs, and more giants beside them. Horsemen, archers, and men with spears, slings, clubs, and leathern shields march on either side of the giants, and bone [chariots](wiki:Chariot) from the [Frozen Shore](wiki:Frozen_Shore) make up the flanks. Once they are within range, the black brothers on the Wall rain down arrows and spiked steel caltrops upon them, until the giants who had been carrying the ram are dead or unable to go on. With fire arrows and barrels of burning oil the advancing host is scared into retreating, giving the day's victory to the Night's Watch.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)

Despite the successful defense of the Night's Watch, the giants below manage to break the outer door of studded [oak](wiki:Oak) of the gate. [Mag Mar Tun Doh Weg](wiki:Mag_Mar_Tun_Doh_Weg) crawls through, withstanding the arrows and reaching the iron gate behind the men of the Night's Watch were standing. Of the five below, Donal is the last defender to die, either killing or mortally wounding Mag in the process by stabbing him in the throat with his sword. Donal dies when the giant crushes his spine.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-8)

Jon, [Pypar](wiki:Pypar), Maester [Aemon](wiki:Aemon_Targaryen_(son_of_Maekar_I)), and [Clydas](wiki:Clydas) enter the tunnel after the battle is over, finding the five dead brothers and the dead giant. Beyond the tunnel, they find the carcass of a dead mammoth partially blocking the entrance, and three more dead giants outside. Jon suggests blocking up the tunnel up to the second gate with rubble, ice, and anything else they can find. However, when he proclaims that Ser Wynton Stout, the last knight remaining at Castle Black, should take over command, Aemon informs him that Wynton is no longer able to do so, and that command has passed to Jon himself.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos64.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-9)

### The turtle

The struggle continues for many days afterwards.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-26) During that time, the Night's Watch runs low of supplies. They have no more oil or barrels of pitch, and are soon to run out of arrows as well. When they receive news from Ser [Denys Mallister](wiki:Denys_Mallister) about the [fight at the Bridge of Skulls](wiki:Fight_at_the_Bridge_of_Skulls), Jon dispatches [Zei](wiki:Zei) to Mole's Town to plead with the villagers to aid the black brothers at the Wall. When she fails to return, Jon sends [Mully](wiki:Mully) after her, who returns to tell them that the entire village has been deserted.(wiki:Battle_beneath_the_Wall.cite_ref-Rasos69.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-2)

## Origins

[Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy), who had captured [Deepwood Motte](wiki:Fall_of_Deepwood_Motte) when [House Greyjoy](wiki:House_Greyjoy) entered the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings), returns to Deepwood after the [kingsmoot](wiki:Kingsmoot). She has four longships and near two hundred men at Deepwood and the strand of the [Bay of Ice](wiki:Bay_of_Ice).(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)

[Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), under the advice of [Jon Snow](wiki:Jon_Snow), goes west from [Castle Black](wiki:Castle_Black) to free Deepwood. While moving through the [northern mountains](wiki:Northern_mountains), Stannis wins the support of the [mountain clans](wiki:Northern_mountain_clans) [Flint](wiki:House_Flint_of_the_mountains), [Wull](wiki:House_Wull), [Norrey](wiki:House_Norrey) and [Liddle](wiki:House_Liddle), who remain loyal to the legacies of Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark) and [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark), the [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North).(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2)(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-10)

## Aftermath

[Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) frees Lady [Sybelle Glover](wiki:Sybelle_Glover) and returns Deepwood Motte to [House Glover](wiki:House_Glover). This wins Stannis much popular support in the [north](wiki:North), with House Glover and its vassal houses as well as [House Mormont](wiki:House_Mormont) declaring for him afterwards.(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-12) Some men sworn to [House Hornwood](wiki:House_Hornwood) also join Stannis' army.(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

Stannis ransoms many of the most notable ironmen,(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4) and only [nine](wiki:Ragged_nine) remain when he departs Deepwood.(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-13) Stannis brings [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy) with him during the king's [march on Winterfell](wiki:March_on_Winterfell), leaving the other captives behind.(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-14) [Morgan Liddle](wiki:Morgan_Liddle) apologizes to Asha for having cursed at her during the fighting.(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-15)

[Cromm](wiki:Cromm) eventually dies of his wounds.(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2) [Tycho Nestoris](wiki:Tycho_Nestoris), an envoy of the [Iron Bank of Braavos](wiki:Iron_Bank_of_Braavos), later ransoms [Tristifer Botley](wiki:Tristifer_Botley), [Fingers](wiki:Fingers_(ironman)), [Grimtongue](wiki:Grimtongue), [Qarl the Maid](wiki:Qarl_the_Maid), [Roggon Rustbeard](wiki:Roggon_Rustbeard), and [Rook](wiki:Rook) from Sybelle's dungeon. Tycho has the six remaining ironmen escort him to Stannis at the [crofters' village](wiki:Crofters'_village).(wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd62.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-3)

## Origins

### Beginning of the Regency[ ]

At the end of the Dance of the Dragons, Aegon III ascended the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) at the age of only ten, having lost most of his family members in the civil war including both of his parents. After witnessing the horrific death of his mother, [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen), late in the war, Aegon had become a quite sullen, melancholic boy who spent days alone brooding. He was used as a pawn as the [lords](wiki:Lord) and [regents](wiki:Regent) of the [small council](wiki:Small_council) all sought to gain a political stronghold.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1)(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rfabaftermath_-_the_hour_of_the_wolf.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)
The regency of King Aegon started with seven individual regents, who became known as a council of seven. Of these seven regents, only [Grand Maester](wiki:Grand_Maester) [Munkun](wiki:Munkun) served during the entire regency. Other regents and [Hands of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King) resigned, were replaced, or died. Aegon's reign began in the second year of a harsh winter, which would last until [135 AC](wiki:135_AC). [Winter Fever](wiki:Winter_Fever) took several of Aegon's regents.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)
The first of Aegon's regents to pass was Lord [Corlys Velaryon](wiki:Corlys_Velaryon), who died in [132 AC](wiki:132_AC) of old age. His grandson and successor, Lord [Alyn Velaryon](wiki:Alyn_Velaryon), hoped to take his grandfather's place on the council but was denied. That same year, Lord [Royce Caron](wiki:Royce_Caron) gave up his place on the council. Ser [Torrhen Manderly](wiki:Torrhen_Manderly) gave up his place on the council as well that year, to return home to [White Harbor](wiki:White_Harbor), following the deaths of his father and brother from Winter Fever.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)
In [133 AC](wiki:133_AC), Winter Fever took Ser [Tyland Lannister](wiki:Tyland_Lannister), who had returned from the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities) the year before, and had been named Hand of the King, a position which he held and fulfilled capably, despite his mutilations. Lord [Roland Westerling](wiki:Roland_Westerling), one of the regents, died in [133 AC](wiki:133_AC) of Winter Fever as well, and was subsequently replaced by Lord [Thaddeus Rowan](wiki:Thaddeus_Rowan).(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4)
Lord [Unwin Peake](wiki:Unwin_Peake) had felt slighted when he had not been amongst the original seven regents. Yet in [132 AC](wiki:132_AC), he was given Lord Corlys Velaryon's position. He rose to the position of Hand in [133 AC](wiki:133_AC), following the death of Ser Tyland Lannister. In his position of power, he saw his kin hold many high office, and endeavored to weaken his rivals by any means at hand.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-5)
King Aegon III had married his cousin, Princess [Jaehaera Targaryen](wiki:Jaehaera_Targaryen), the sole surviving child of King [Aegon II Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_II_Targaryen), following the end of the civil war. She apparently killed herself in [133 AC](wiki:133_AC), when she threw herself out of her window and was impaled on the spikes of [Maegor's Holdfast](wiki:Maegor's_Holdfast). Whilst officially named a suicide, there are some who question the manner of her death. Some whisper that Jaehaera had been murdered, and one of the suspects named was Ser [Mervyn Flowers](wiki:Mervyn_Flowers) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), the [bastard](wiki:Bastard) half-brother of Lord Unwin, who had been the guard at Jaehaera's door when she died. [Court-fool](wiki:Fool) [Mushroom](wiki:Mushroom) suggests that Mervyn did not do the deed himself, but had allowed for someone else to push her out of the window. Maester [Yandel](wiki:Yandel) speculates the assassin was [Tessario the Tiger](wiki:Tessario), a [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) of the Free Cities in Lord Unwin's service.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-6)
Following the death of Queen Jaehaera, Lord Unwin attempted to marry his own daughter to King Aegon. When the regents stopped his attempts, the Hand arranged the [Maiden's Day Ball](wiki:Maiden's_Day_Ball), during which a thousand maids were presented to the king. Ladies [Rhaena](wiki:Rhaena_Targaryen_(daughter_of_Daemon)) and [Baela Targaryen](wiki:Baela_Targaryen) presented a cousin of theirs, Lady [Daenaera Velaryon](wiki:Daenaera_Velaryon), at the time only six years old. Aegon choose her as his new queen, which greatly frustrated Lord Peake. He attempted to have the king's choice put aside, but the other regents, as well as Aegon, opposed him. Unwin threatened to resign as Hand of the King in an attempt to bend the other regents to his will, but they were only happy to oblige. Thus Lord Peake's resignation was accepted and he was replaced as Hand by Lord [Thaddeus Rowan](wiki:Thaddeus_Rowan) in [134 AC](wiki:134_AC).(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-7)

### The Red Kraken[ ]

Lord [Dalton Greyjoy](wiki:Dalton_Greyjoy), the Red Kraken, had joined the [Dance of the Dragons](wiki:Dance_of_the_Dragons) on the side of Queen [Rhaenyra Targaryen](wiki:Rhaenyra_Targaryen), and had been allowed to raid the western coasts.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_ii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Though the war ended, [House Greyjoy](wiki:House_Greyjoy)'s raids from the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands) did not.
Lord [Alyn Velaryon](wiki:Alyn_Velaryon), denied Lord [Corlys Velaryon](wiki:Corlys_Velaryon)'s place on the council, sailed with Ser [Gedmund Peake](wiki:Gedmund_Peake) against [Racallio Ryndoon](wiki:Racallio_Ryndoon) in the [Stepstones](wiki:Stepstones). While Gedmund waited at [Tarth](wiki:Tarth) for orders from Unwin, the Velaryons continued south and achieved a [great victory](wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack) against Racallio's [Braavosi](wiki:Braavos) allies.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) When Alyn returned to King's Landing, his newborn fame proved divisive. The regents, despite Unwin's protests, gave Alyn honors and rewards. Lord Peake in the end convinced the regents to dispatch Alyn to the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) to deal with Dalton, almost certainly hoping it would result in Alyn's death or defeat. Instead, it became the first of six great voyages by Alyn.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_aegon_iii.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-8)
Dalton was eventually killed on [Fair Isle](wiki:Fair_Isle), when a girl named [Tess](wiki:Tess) opened his throat with a dagger. As Dalton had never married, a bloody struggle began within hours of his death. Lady [Johanna Lannister](wiki:Johanna_Lannister) extracted revenge for all the suffering of the westerlands in [134 AC](wiki:134_AC), when a Lannister force, with the aid of Ser [Leo Costayne](wiki:Leo_Costayne), attacked the Iron Islands.(wiki:Regency_of_Aegon_III.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_iron_islands:_the_red_kraken.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)
